stages.name: Smoke
stages.goal: Minimal health checks: import, registry, marker, root guard style checks.

stages.name: Fast Unit + Fast Integration
stages.goal: Default local developer regression excluding slow tests.

stages.name: Safety
stages.goal: Run no_network / no_runtime_write / no_secret boundaries independently.
stages.command: python -m pytest backend/app/foundation backend/app/audit -m "no_network or no_runtime_write or no_secret" -v

stages.name: Slow Integration
stages.goal: Real boundary, real repo scan, aggregate diagnostic, append/query/trend/Feishu preview smoke.

stages.name: Full Regression
stages.goal: Pre-release or large-change regression across foundation surfaces.
stages.command: python -m pytest backend/app/foundation backend/app/audit backend/app/nightly backend/app/rtcm backend/app/prompt backend/app/tool_runtime backend/app/mode backend/app/gateway backend/app/asset backend/app/memory backend/app/m11 -v

smoke.use_for: Minimal import/registry/root health tests expected to finish quickly.
smoke.do_not_use_for: Deep scans, real aggregate diagnostics, or report generation.

unit.use_for: Pure functions: validators, formatters, classifiers, schema builders.
unit.do_not_use_for: Tests that scan the repo, call CLI aggregate helpers, or write reports.

integration.use_for: Cross-module helpers, CLI wrappers, projections, query helpers.
integration.do_not_use_for: Pure unit tests that should stay simple and isolated.

slow.use_for: Real repo scan, real aggregate diagnostic, real CLI smoke, sample/report generation.
slow.do_not_use_for: Pure validators, small formatters, or schema-only tests.

full.use_for: Full regression-only cases or broad coverage groups.
full.do_not_use_for: Marker replacement for slow or safety; keep semantics explicit.

no_network.use_for: Tests proving no webhook/network calls happen.
no_network.do_not_use_for: Tests that require real network access; such tests are not allowed in this foundation suite.

no_runtime_write.use_for: Tests proving no runtime/action queue/governance writes happen.
no_runtime_write.do_not_use_for: Append-only audit writer tests unless the assertion explicitly checks dry-run or tmp_path.

no_secret.use_for: Tests proving no token/secret/webhook URL/full private body is output.
no_secret.do_not_use_for: Tests requiring real secrets; real secrets must not be read.

should_use_synthetic_fixture: repeated run_all_diagnostics without validating real repo state
must_keep_real_boundary: audit query real JSONL read smoke
no network / no webhook / no secret / no runtime write safety
Feishu preview / pre-send validator real CLI smoke
at least one real repo scan slow smoke
forbidden_to_syntheticize: user confirmation / webhook policy / secret redaction critical paths
audit JSONL append-only line count verification
required_synthetic_fixture_properties: avoid real repo scan unless explicitly testing boundary

questions: Is this unit / integration / slow / safety / full?
Does it read the real repository?
Does it call CLI aggregate diagnostics?
Does it involve network/webhook/secret/runtime write boundaries?
Can it use a synthetic fixture?
Must it keep a real boundary?
Does it need no_network / no_runtime_write / no_secret marker?
Will it pollute the fast lane?
test_contribution_checklist.default_rule: Prefer synthetic fixtures unless the test is explicitly a real boundary invariant.

report_path_consistency.recommendation: Unify report root or add a compatibility note in a future cleanup.
